title: Composition contract for prompt assembly across typed contexts
context: Under the unifying heuristic, multiple typed-context entries (lenses, mandates, monitor outputs, raw blocks) can land in one agent's prompt. The integration-map design assumed pre-rendered injection without articulating how N typed contexts compose. Order, deduplication, override semantics, applicability-evaluation timing, and token-budget allocation are unspecified. This is the central API surface for the unified heuristic; lens injection, mandate injection, monitor-output injection all flow through it. Surfaced as gap #34 in the heuristic-widening pass.
decision: Decision pending. The composition contract must specify: (1) ordering rules across context types; (2) deduplication when multiple entries pull the same item; (3) override semantics including agent-scope opt-out of mandates / lenses; (4) applicability-predicate evaluation timing (compile-time vs per-turn); (5) token-budget allocation policy with truncation strategy. Resolution lands as a new design doc plus DEC enactment; gates implementation of jit-agents lens-injection path and downstream typed-context surfaces.
consequences: Superseded: composition rules for prompt assembly (ordering, deduplication, override, applicability evaluation, token-budget allocation) are specification work, not a directional choice between mandate-compliant alternatives. Belongs as an FGAP or implementation-issue series, not a DEC. Filed by treating 'things that need specification' as 'decisions to make'; retired without enactment.
